schema_version.description: Schema version. Do not modify manually.

analyzed_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this analysis was performed.

analyzed_commit.description: Full SHA of the commit that was analyzed.

analyzed_by.description: Identifier of the agent/tool that performed analysis.

path.description: Path to MCP config file relative to repository root. Examples: ".mcp.json", ".mcps/.sequential-thinking.mcp.json", ".mcp-tool-sets/all/.mcp.json"

server_count.description: Number of MCP servers configured in this file.

purpose.description: Brief description of this config file's purpose if identifiable. Examples: "Sequential thinking only", "Full discovery stack", "Semantic code search"

servers.description: MCP server entries found in this configuration file. For files with many servers (>10), list a representative sample and note total in server_count.

name.description: MCP server name or identifier as configured.

command.description: Command used to launch this MCP server if specified.

items.description: A single MCP server configuration entry.

config_files.description: MCP configuration files found in the repository.

config_directories.description: Directories containing MCP configuration files. Examples: [".mcps/", ".mcp-tool-sets/"]

total_servers.description: Total number of unique MCP servers configured across all files.

mcp_section.description: MCP tool configuration found in the repository.

path.description: Path to script file relative to repository root.

mode.description: How this script invokes the AI CLI.

summary.description: Brief description of what this script does.

configures_mcp.description: Whether this script configures or passes MCP server arguments (--mcp-config).

ai_cli_scripts.description: Bash scripts and automation that invoke AI CLI tools.

path.description: Path to template folder or file relative to repository root.

location_type.description: Classification of where these templates live.

template_count.description: Number of template files found in this collection.

naming_convention.description: Naming convention observed if identifiable. Examples: "NN-name-template.md", "prompt_v{N}.md", "{domain}-{task}.md"

summary.description: Brief description of what these templates are for.

inferred_target_artifacts.description: Artifact types this collection likely produces. A single collection may target multiple types (e.g., a playbook with steps for readme + claude_md + field_guide). Omit for work_folder and inline collections.

items.description: Artifact type this template collection likely produces. Infer from directory names, template names (e.g., "update-readme", "claude-md-hierarchy"), and frontmatter purpose fields. A collection may target multiple artifact types.

snippet_extractions.description: AI tooling, tech stack, and mandate signals extracted from reading the template content. Only populate for snippet and playbook location types.

tech_stack_references.description: Tech stack signals extracted from reading this collection's content. Include framework names and versions where specified. Examples: "React 19.1", "Ink 6.4 terminal UI", "LangGraph v1 TypeScript", "Next.js App Router", "Python 3.11", "PostgreSQL via psql CLI"

ai_tooling.description: AI tooling references extracted: MCP server names, AI CLI tools, and specific tool-use directives found in template instructions. Include the directive context, not just the tool name. Examples: "mcp__sequential-thinking for complex analysis", "mcp__docs-langchain — MUST USE to verify LangGraph v1 syntax", "psql CLI mandatory for all database queries", "playwright MCP for UI verification after changes"

mandate_patterns.description: Key mandate/rule patterns extracted (NEVER/ALWAYS/MANDATORY/CRITICAL rules). Summarize each as a compact rule statement preserving the intent. Examples: "NEVER use inline functions in JSX props — causes re-render cycles", "Always use psql CLI to query the database", "MANDATORY: verify LangGraph v1 syntax with mcp__docs-langchain before generating", "Hooks must be called at top level of components — React Rules of Hooks"

metrics.description: Volume and temporal metrics for this template collection.

file_count.description: Number of files in this scope.

line_count.description: Total lines across all files in this scope.

last_commit_date.description: Date of the most recent commit touching files in this scope. Format: YYYY-MM-DD

last_commit_sha.description: Short SHA of the most recent commit touching files in this scope.

prompt_template_collections.description: Prompt template collections found in the repository. Includes organized template folders, playbooks, snippets, and work-folder .prompts/ directories.

path.description: Path to CLAUDE.md file relative to repository root.

scope.description: Scope this CLAUDE.md applies to. Examples: "root", "packages/core", "docs/projects"

has_nontrivial_rules.description: Whether this file contains non-trivial rules beyond basic project description. Examples of non-trivial: custom commit conventions, tool restrictions, code style mandates, agent workflow instructions.

line_count.description: Line count of this CLAUDE.md file.

claude_md_files.description: CLAUDE.md files found in the repository.

uses_hierarchical_claude_md.description: Whether the repo uses CLAUDE.md files in subdirectories (not just root).

agents.description: Claude Code agent definitions found (typically in .claude/agents/ or similar).

path.description: Path to definition file relative to repository root.

name.description: Name or identifier if extractable from filename or content.

items.description: A named definition file (agent, command, skill, or sub-agent).

commands.description: Claude Code slash command definitions found (typically in .claude/commands/).

path.description: Path to definition file relative to repository root.

name.description: Name or identifier if extractable from filename or content.

items.description: A named definition file (agent, command, skill, or sub-agent).

path.description: Path to definition file relative to repository root.

name.description: Name or identifier if extractable from filename or content.

items.description: A named definition file (agent, command, skill, or sub-agent).

path.description: Path to definition file relative to repository root.

name.description: Name or identifier if extractable from filename or content.

items.description: A named definition file (agent, command, skill, or sub-agent).

claude_config.description: Claude-specific agent configuration (CLAUDE.md, agents, commands, skills, sub-agents).

snippet_catalog.description: Cross-collection summary of AI tooling and tech stack signals extracted from deep-reading snippet and playbook template files. Omit if no snippet/playbook collections found.

tech_stack_signals.description: Deduplicated tech stack signals across all analyzed snippet/playbook collections. Cross-reference against code-structure analysis for validation — signals that match the repo's active tech stack are high-confidence; orphaned signals suggest historical or aspirational templates.

ai_tooling_signals.description: Deduplicated AI tooling signals across all analyzed snippet/playbook collections. Includes MCP server names, CLI tools, and specific tool-use mandates. These complement mcp_section (config-based) with instruction-based tooling references.

analyzed_collection_count.description: Number of template collections that were read and analyzed for signal extraction (snippet and playbook types only).

ai_generated_doc_evidence.description: Evidence of AI-generated standard documentation (README, CLAUDE.md, conceptual docs). Cross-referenced against conceptual-docs output when provided as jagged input. Omit if no evidence found.

readme_indicators.description: Evidence that README.md was created or structured by an AI prompt template. Look for: readme update/generation templates, shields.io badge conventions in templates, template parameters ({{app-name}}, {{version}}) that correlate with the README structure. Examples: "readme update template found at prompts/82-update-readme/", "shields.io badge pattern in template matches repo README structure"

claude_md_indicators.description: Evidence that the CLAUDE.md hierarchy was created by an AI prompt template. Look for: CLAUDE.md generation templates, templates referencing hierarchical scope parameters ({{app-folder}}, subdirectory CLAUDE.md outputs), "minimal root / focused areas" structural patterns in templates matching the actual CLAUDE.md files. Examples: "CLAUDE.md hierarchy generation template found at step-23/", "Template output locations match actual CLAUDE.md file paths"

conceptual_doc_indicators.description: Evidence that specific conceptual doc types (index, glossary, user-manual, field-guide) were created by AI prompt templates. List each doc type separately with evidence. Cross-reference against conceptual-docs analysis output when available: if has_field_guide is true and a field-guide generation template exists, that's strong evidence. Examples: "field-guide generation template at step-24/ — docs/field-guide.md present in repo", "user-manual template references docs/user-manual.md output — file confirmed present"

has_ai_cli_scripts.description: Has bash scripts invoking AI CLIs

has_snippets.description: Has snippet collections that were deep-read for AI tooling and tech stack signals

has_ai_generated_doc_evidence.description: Found template evidence of AI-generated standard docs (README, CLAUDE.md, conceptual docs)

patterns.description: AI process patterns observed. Examples: "Headless scripts use --dangerously-skip-permissions with session tracking", "MCP configs organized by purpose in .mcps/ directory", "Playbooks follow numbered step sequence (NN-name-template.md)", "Repo-specific snippets cover tech stack mandates for React/Ink components"

metrics.description: Aggregate volume and temporal metrics across all AI process files.

file_count.description: Number of files in this scope.

line_count.description: Total lines across all files in this scope.

last_commit_date.description: Date of the most recent commit touching files in this scope. Format: YYYY-MM-DD

last_commit_sha.description: Short SHA of the most recent commit touching files in this scope.

notes.description: Additional observations, assumptions, or mismatches discovered during analysis. Document any ambiguity in classification that could not be resolved. Note if AI-related source code was found but excluded (e.g., LLM provider integrations in app code). Note if jagged inputs (code-structure, conceptual-docs) were or were not available.

description: Repository AI process inventory analysis output (v1.2.0). Catalogs MCP configurations, AI CLI scripts, prompt templates, playbooks, and agent configuration. For snippet and playbook collections, extracts AI tooling signals, tech stack references, and mandate patterns. Detects evidence of AI-generated standard documentation artifacts. Pure inventory for downstream analysis — no recommendations.
